metaTitle: Piscataway Pest Control | Same-Day Service Available
metaDescription: Expert pest control in Piscataway, NJ. Serving Rutgers campus, Hoes Lane, and River Road properties. Fast response for rodents, bed bugs & more. Call today!
introParagraph: Piscataway's mix of Rutgers University housing, commercial centers along Hoes Lane, and residential neighborhoods near the Raritan River creates diverse pest challenges. From older homes in Randolphville to modern apartments along River Road and office complexes near Centennial Avenue, our certified technicians deliver targeted pest solutions. We serve properties throughout the township, including areas near the New Jersey Turnpike and Route 287 corridor. Same-day emergency service available for urgent infestations affecting Piscataway homes and businesses.
whyThisAreaSection: Piscataway's location between the Raritan River and South Branch creates moisture issues that attract pests year-round. The township's blend of established neighborhoods from the 1960s-70s, active student housing near Rutgers Busch and Livingston campuses, and commercial development along Route 287 generates constant pest pressure. Proximity to wooded areas along Johnson Park and aging infrastructure in older sections creates entry points for rodents and insects. Heavy commuter traffic along Interstate 287 and the Turnpike corridor introduces pests to hotels and businesses along Centennial Avenue.
propertyTypesSection: Piscataway features split-level homes and ranch properties in neighborhoods like Woodbridge Knolls and Possumtown, where basement foundation gaps and crawl spaces allow rodent entry. Garden-style apartment complexes near Rutgers experience shared-wall bed bug and cockroach spread. Student housing along Bartholomew Road faces high turnover introducing pests. Office parks and medical facilities along Hoes Lane deal with moisture-related ant problems. Townhome communities near New Market suffer from attic wildlife intrusions. Strip malls along Stelton Road attract pests to dumpster areas and loading docks.
commonPestsSection: Mice and rats dominate in Piscataway, entering through foundation cracks in older homes and via utility penetrations in apartment buildings. German cockroaches spread rapidly through multi-family student housing near campus. Bed bugs appear frequently in high-turnover rentals. Carpenter ants exploit moisture damage near Raritan River properties. Termites threaten wooden structures in established neighborhoods. Squirrels and raccoons invade attics in wooded areas. Wasps nest under eaves during summer months.

faqs.question: How much does pest control cost in Piscataway?
faqs.answer: General pest control costs $175–$300 per treatment in Piscataway. Cockroach or ant extermination runs $350–$550. Mouse or rat removal ranges from $350–$600, while bed bug heat or chemical treatments cost $1,200–$3,500 depending on infestation severity and property size. Emergency same-day service ranges from $350–$1,000.

faqs.question: How quickly can you respond to pest emergencies in Piscataway?
faqs.answer: We offer same-day emergency service throughout Piscataway, including Rutgers campus areas, Hoes Lane commercial district, and residential neighborhoods near River Road. Our technicians typically arrive within 2-4 hours for urgent rodent, bed bug, or stinging insect situations. We maintain service vehicles strategically positioned to reach properties off Route 287 and the Turnpike corridor quickly.

faqs.question: Are your treatments safe for children and pets in my home?
faqs.answer: Absolutely. We use EPA-approved products with proven safety profiles for occupied Piscataway homes and apartments. Our technicians apply targeted treatments in cracks, crevices, and pest harborage areas—not broad sprays. We provide specific re-entry times (typically 2-4 hours) and pet precautions. Child-safe bait stations are used for rodent control in family homes.

faqs.question: Why do I keep seeing mice near my Piscataway foundation?
faqs.answer: Older Piscataway homes, especially split-levels and ranches built in the 1960s-70s, develop foundation settling cracks and basement window gaps. Mice only need a dime-sized opening to enter. Proximity to wooded areas and the Raritan River provides rodent populations nearby. Our service includes entry point identification and exclusion recommendations to prevent recurring infestations.

faqs.question: How do bed bugs spread in Rutgers area apartments?
faqs.answer: Student housing near Rutgers campuses experiences high resident turnover, with used furniture and frequent moving introducing bed bugs. These pests travel through shared walls, electrical conduits, and plumbing chases in garden-style complexes. Apartment buildings along Bartholomew Road and River Road are particularly vulnerable. Early detection and treatment of adjacent units prevents building-wide infestations.

faqs.question: Do you service commercial properties along Hoes Lane and Centennial Avenue?
faqs.answer: Yes, we provide comprehensive commercial pest control for Piscataway's business corridors. Our services cover office parks, medical facilities, hotels, and retail centers along Hoes Lane, Centennial Avenue, and Stelton Road. We offer discreet treatments, flexible scheduling around business hours, and documentation for health inspections. Monthly maintenance programs prevent pest disruptions to your operations.
